revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Akōbō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:阿光坊古墳群]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofuns in Oirase, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Akōbō Kofun Group | native_name = 阿光坊古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Akobo Kofun.jpg; File:Akobo Kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Akōbō Kofun group | map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Akōbō Kofun group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|40|36|28|N|141|22|40|E||display=inline|qq=}}; {{Coord|40.609444|N|141.376111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Oirase, Aomori|Oirase, Aomori]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Oirase|qid=Q1349124|1=Oirase}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 112,738 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th to 9th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1989-1990 | archaeologists = Oirase Town Board of Education | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum on site) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Akōbō Kofun group'''|阿光坊古墳群| Akōbō kofun-gun|qq=}} is a large group of circular ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the town of [[:en:Oirase, Aomori|Oirase]], in {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|en|Kamikita District, Aomori|qid=Q1143970|lt=Kamikita District|1=Kamikita District, Aomori}} of {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}} in the far northern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It has protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since July 29, 2007 .<ref>[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00003536 Akōbō Kofun Cluster]. [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== Located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} on the left bank of the {{ill|Oirase River|en|Oirase River|qid=Q1156064|lt=Oirase River|1=Oirase River}}, approximately seven kilometers from the present-day {{ill|Pacific Ocean|en|Pacific Ocean|qid=Q98|lt=Pacific coast|1=Pacific Ocean}} at an altitude of 30 to 40 meters, this group of ancient tombs was variously known as the "Tenjinyama site" or the "Jusanmori site", and was believed by locals in pre-modern times to contain the graves of warriors, as evidenced by occasional fragments of swords and pottery which were uncovered by plowing nearby fields. After the discovery of ''[[magatama]]'' beads in April 1986, the site was extensively excavated between 1989 and 1990 by the local Oirase Town Board of Education, and were discovered to consist of 108 domed ''kofun'' dating from the first half of the 7th century to the early 9th century AD. Many of the tombs had a shallow circumferential groove resembling a small moat. The tombs contained the remnants of swords, agricultural tools, beads and other jewelry articles, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, and {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|qid=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}es and the remains of wooden {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. Many of the items appear to have originated from the southern part of the Tohoku region, indicating frequent trade. The size of each tomb was approximately 10 meters in diameter, with a height of 1.1 meter. As the mounds are now flattened and do not appear above the present ground-level, a possibility exists that more undiscovered tombs exist in the area. Approximately 3 kilometers east of this {{ill|Necropolis|en|Necropolis|qid=Q200141|lt=Necropolis|1=Necropolis}} is the Nakanodaira archeological site, where more than 100 [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s were discovered. The Negishi archeological site, which was the site of a large pit dwelling, was also nearby, suggesting that the people of lived in the locations had some relationship with the construction of the tombs. In 2016, the town of Oirase completed construction of a local museum at the site. It is located approximately 10 minutes by car from the [[:en:Aoimori Railway|Aoimori Railway]] {{ill|Mukaiyama Station|en|Mukaiyama Station|qid=Q5357175|lt=Mukaiyama Station|1=Mukaiyama Station}}. ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|qq=}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|qid=Q6573255|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.town.oirase.aomori.jp/site/kofun/ Oirase town home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/kinen_siseki_18.html Aomori Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Akōbō Kofun Cluster]]
